title: Vibrant Kenyan Chicken Stew
description: A fresh, crisp, low-sodium Kenyan chicken stew featuring canned tomatoes and tangy nuka-pickle, served over a hot-stone plate.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced onion, garlic, and ginger; sauté until the onion turns translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the ground cumin and cook for 30 seconds to release the spices.
Add the chicken pieces and brown them on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the canned tomatoes, low-sodium chicken broth, and lemon juice, then bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover the skillet, and simmer for 25 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Stir in the chopped nuka-pickle and cook for an additional 5 minutes, just to warm the pickles and blend the flavors.
Taste and season with a pinch of black pepper if desired; transfer the stew onto a preheated hot-stone plate and serve hot.
